## Permablog
![permablog_icon.jpg](https://arweave.net/zS0dJvhWRYfDSekbNuZvPUcYnJ1ta6OoiBpa2A6YQ80 'permablog_icon.jpg')

### TLDR;

**Spiritual successor to Permanotes**

- permanently store notes on Arweave
- permanece first, with optional services (mailing, monetisation, discovery etc.)

### Why now?

- The world had been foucsed on growth/scaling for the last 10-20 years
- Example of that would be services like Substack, which puts its focus on scaling audience and monetisation
- Current economic downturn will lead to:
  - less money to be made
  - less growth/scaling
  - more value preservation (gold prices)
  - Conservatism on the rise
- Shift in ideology brings behavioural change
- Permanence is and always will be valuable
- Take advantage of the shift in ideology
- We need more apps that put permanence at the center stage
- What better place to start than note taking and blog writing

### Existing services

#### Paragraph.xyz

- branded as the onchain version of Substack
- brand is tightly coupled to blockchain/crypto. A lot of people are writing apps off just because they are associated with crypto
- has Arweave as an optional storage provider but it's lost in a sea of other features

#### Permanotes

- Lacks design and optional services. Just storing things permanently is not enough.

### Other Features

- Very simple login to start blogging
- Arweave native (fully onchain) with offchain plugins (publishing, monetisation, discovery etc.) for scalability
- Quick Wallet for seamless account creation
- In-app turbo credits purchase (abstracted away to make it simpler)
- headless first, with optional discovery page in the site
  - Why?
    - platforms like substack have limited/no support for programmaticly fetching and embedding articles in the author's own site. We can cover that part of the market.

### Design

- revert back to a more realism-based, almost ios 4 skeuomorphic design
- move away from abstract minimalism

### Business model

- Service fee when purchasing credits for media uploads
   
### Why this makes sense for us now?
- Take advantage of the shift in ideology
- It lets us use Arweave-native tech to power the Arweave.org site
- Part of our bigger campaign of Arweave-first, having high-quality Arweave apps

### The here and now
- Article editing, publishing and fetching
- Brand design and identity
- UI design

### Moving forward
- extension services
  - mailing
  - monetisation
  - discovery
